Title: MiR396-GRF module associates with switchgrass biomass yield and feedstock quality

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall recalcitrance/feedstock quality and sugar release for biofuel production
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-transcriptional repression of GRF-mediated control of lignin and cell wall carbohydrate biosynthesis associated with biomass yield
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PvGRF1; PvGRF3; PvGRF4; PvGRF5; PvGRF6; PvGRF8; PvGRF9
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses/cleaves PvGRF transcripts; indirectly downregulates PvGA20ox2, Pv4CL and PvCCR and perturbs genes in auxin, glucose and xylan biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces plant height, biomass yield, lignin content mainly through reduced G-lignin, and often reduces glucose content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative master regulator within the miR396-GRF module affecting feedstock quality and biomass-associated sugar yield
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous overexpression of Osa-MIR396a in switchgrass; qRT-PCR; lignin and sugar profiling; phenotyping; complementation with PvGRF9/rPvGRF9; 5' RLM-RACE for target cleavage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR396 overexpression caused dwarfism and lower biomass yield in switchgrass. 2. It reduced stem lignin content mainly by lowering G-lignin monomer content and often reduced cell wall glucose content. 3. It cleaved multiple PvGRFs and acted largely through repression of PvGRF9 and PvGRF1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height and dry biomass yield; narrower/shorter leaves; lower lignin and glucose content
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth to R3 stage; enzymatic hydrolysis assessed with and without NaOH pretreatment
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback indicated because PvGRF9 overexpression reduced miR396 level in OE17 for wild-type PvGRF9 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct upregulation is not desirable for biofuel feedstock because it lowers biomass yield despite lowering lignin
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The overexpressed precursor was from rice, but the metabolic effects were evaluated in switchgrass; miR396 expression was ubiquitous and highest in mature florets
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ubiquitous in flower, stem and leaf; highest in mature florets

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Growth-Regulating Factor (GRF) family with WRC and QLQ motifs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: determines feedstock quality, saccharification behavior and sugar output for biofuel production
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: regulation of stem elongation and cell wall lignin/glucose/xylan-associated biosynthetic programs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PvGA20ox2; PvYUCC2; Pv4CL; PvCCR; PvPAL; PvCSLA6/PvCSLC2; PvIRX9; PvIRX14; PvCESA1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively associated with expression of PvGA20ox2, Pv4CL and PvCCR; module negatively regulates PvYUCC2; perturbs glucose/xylan biosynthesis-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of plant height, dry biomass and lignin content mainly via G-lignin; overexpression increases per-plant sugar yield after pretreatment despite lower saccharification efficiency per biomass
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Dominant GRF effector in the miR396-GRF module; sufficient to complement OE-miR396 defects and most strongly linked to biomass/feedstock traits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rPvGRF9 overexpression; PvGRF9-SRDX repression; complementation in OE17; qRT-PCR; lignin monomer analysis; glucose yield and enzymatic hydrolysis assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvGRF9 is highly expressed in mature stem and is directly cleaved by miR396. 2. Overexpression increases plant height, biomass yield and lignin content, whereas SRDX repression causes dwarfism and reduced G-lignin/glucose. 3. Overexpression of PvGRF9 or miR396-resistant rPvGRF9 rescues OE-miR396 phenotypes, and rPvGRF9 raises per-plant soluble sugar yield by increasing biomass.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher lignin and G-lignin reduce enzymatic hydrolysis efficiency per biomass without pretreatment; potential feedstock recalcitrance increase
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth to R3 stage; saccharification evaluated with and without alkaline pretreatment
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedback on miR396 and other PvGRFs in complementation lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering switchgrass with higher biomass and per-plant sugar yield; useful especially when paired with pretreatment-based biofuel processing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Wild-type PvGRF9 overexpression in OE17 reduced miR396 level, whereas rPvGRF9 did not, suggesting indirect network effects; stronger effect than PvGRF1

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Growth-Regulating Factor (GRF) family with WRC and QLQ motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: regulation of stem elongation and cell wall composition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of plant height and likely lignin/glucose accumulation; repression reduces plant height, biomass and cell wall glucose
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant positive regulator with PvGRF9 for stem growth and lignin-associated feedstock quality, but weaker than PvGRF9
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rPvGRF1 overexpression; PvGRF1-SRDX repression; qRT-PCR; phenotyping; lignin/glucose assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvGRF1 is highly expressed in mature stems and is cleaved by miR396. 2. Overexpression increases plant height, while SRDX repression reduces height and biomass. 3. Lignin content shows a positive correlation trend with PvGRF1 expression and repression lowers glucose yield.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely increased lignin may reduce saccharification per biomass, but this was less fully quantified than for PvGRF9
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary engineering target for biomass improvement, possibly in combination with PvGRF9
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with PvGRF9 but with weaker phenotypic impact
